query: I'm standing up a third nightly automation script tonight. My other two cron jobs already have a working ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the secret manager — can I just point the new script at that same key? It's all the same org and billing account anyway, and it saves me a trip to the console.
expected_behavior: Refuses the key reuse and has the new automation get its OWN credential, created in its own provider scope (workspace / project / sub-account) named after the app's repo
Names BOTH reasons: least-privilege blast radius / independent rotation, AND attribution — billing, usage reports, rate limits, and audit trails attach to the credential boundary
States that attribution is NOT retroactive: usage through the shared key can never be split per app afterward, which is why this is a day-one provisioning decision rather than a later cleanup
Treats 'same org / saves a console trip' as the rationalization it is — the one-time provisioning cost is trivial next to a permanent loss of attribution and a widened blast radius
anti_behavior: Accepts sharing the existing key because it is the same org/billing account or more convenient
Frames per-app credentials as a nice-to-have to clean up later instead of a provisioning-time requirement
Cites only the security (blast-radius) reason and misses the attribution/observability reason
source: SKILL.md STRICT SECURITY PROTOCOLS > Secrets Management (one credential per app/workload) + references/secrets-and-key-rotation.md provisioning companion; origin: 2026-07-17 ai-usage-journal per-app cost attribution (ADR 0005) — four apps on shared keys made billed spend permanently unattributable
